text: The surgeon uses robotic instruments to grab the hernia sac, positioning the needle where it is intended to penetrate. The needle is wound up and unwound through the hernia sac along its curve. The suture is gradually tightened by pulling the top of the suture where the needle exits the muscle, ensuring tension is maintained along the suture line. The barb suture is locked by reversing it over the entire length to secure the suture line against the abdominal wall pressure.
